extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. The only transition that sets sent:=true is [t_send_allowed] (on send_attempt), which is guarded by "not opted_out". opted_out becomes true only via [t_optout], which is monotonic — no transition ever sets opted_out:=false — and that same [t_optout] simultaneously sets sent:=false. Therefore once opted_out is true: (a) it stays true forever, (b) any send_attempt routes to [t_send_blocked] which sets sent:=false, and (c) every key_* transition also sets sent:=false. There is no transition that can set sent:=true while opted_out is true, so the conjunction opted_out=true AND sent=true is unreachable. The lock/combination (stages 0..6 advanced by key_0,key_2,key_2,key_0,key_2,key_1) is irrelevant to the property: send_attempt is gated only by opted_out, never by stage, so the lock never enables a send. The property holds regardless of lock state.
